Name: Minimum Temperature
Definition: Average daily minimum temperature for the month
Notes: Available for historical (1970-2000) and CMIP6 future scenarios

Name: Maximum Temperature
Definition: Average daily maximum temperature for the month
Notes: Available for historical (1970-2000) and CMIP6 future scenarios

Name: Average Temperature
Definition: Mean of tmin and tmax for the month
Notes: Historical only; not distributed for CMIP6 future data

Name: Precipitation
Notes: Available for historical (1970-2000) and CMIP6 future scenarios; kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Wind Speed
Definition: Average 10 m wind speed for the month

Name: Water Vapour Pressure
Definition: Average water vapour partial pressure for the month

Name: Mean daily maximum air temperature
Definition: Mean daily maximum 2 m air temperature

Name: Mean daily minimum air temperature
Definition: Mean daily minimum 2 m air temperature

Name: Precipitation amount
Definition: Precipitation of water in all phases; monthly total
Notes: CHELSA v2.1 (1981-2010); kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3)

Name: Surface downwelling shortwave flux in air
Definition: Shortwave flux at the surface; attenuating effects of clouds accounted for
Notes: CHELSA v2.1 (1981-2010); confirmed 404 at CHELSA's server as of 2026-07-24 — not currently fetchable regardless of unit

Name: Total cloud cover at surface
Definition: Cloud cover across the whole atmospheric column seen from the surface upwards

Name: Climate moisture index
Notes: CHELSA v2.1 (1981-2010); kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow

Name: Potential evapotranspiration
Definition: PET calculated with the Penman-Monteith equation
Notes: CHELSA v2.1 (1981-2010); files are named pet_penman; kg->L conversion performed using the water-density identity (1 kg/m^2 ≈ 1 L/m^2 ≈ 1 mm, density ~1000 kg/m^3); this is a liquid water equivalent, not the actual density of vapour or snow
